title: SSL Stripping Attack
description: Downgrading HTTPS connections to HTTP to intercept encrypted traffic.
fullExplanation: SSL Stripping is a MITM attack that downgrades HTTPS to HTTP. The attacker sits between victim and server. When the victim requests http://bank.com, the server redirects to https://bank.com. The attacker intercepts this redirect, maintains HTTPS with the server, but serves HTTP to the victim. The victim sees http:// in the address bar but may not notice. All traffic is now plaintext to the attacker.

attackSteps.description: Use ARP spoofing or rogue WiFi to intercept traffic
Attacker intercepts redirect, keeps HTTPS with server, sends HTTP to victim
Victim's browser shows http://, sends plaintext credentials
Attacker encrypts and forwards to server, server thinks everything is normal

keyFacts: Only works if user initially requests HTTP (not HTTPS)
Victim sees http:// in address bar (but may not notice)
Browser warnings about 'Not Secure' help users notice
Modern browsers increasingly default to HTTPS
Certificate pinning doesn't help (no certificate involved)
requirements: Victim must initially request HTTP (not HTTPS)
No HSTS policy on target domain
Victim doesn't notice http:// in address bar
